description: Implement the About (What is Kiro?), Features, and How Kiro Works pages with full content, animations, and responsive design.
steps: Create app/about/page.tsx - 'What is Kiro?' page with: (1) Introduction hero section explaining Kiro as an AI-native IDE, (2) Product philosophy section - why Kiro exists and its vision, (3) Specification-driven development explanation with visual diagrams/illustrations using styled divs, (4) AI agent overview explaining how Kiro's AI agents work, (5) Key differentiators section. Export metadata with title 'What is Kiro? | Kiro Hub' and appropriate description. Use semantic HTML throughout.

Create app/features/page.tsx - Features page with interactive feature cards. Features to showcase: AI Specifications, Autonomous Development, Task Planning, Context Awareness, GitHub Integration, PR Automation, Multi-file Editing, Terminal Automation, Agent Memory, Web Automation. Each card should have an icon (lucide-react), title, description, and hover/click interaction using framer-motion. Export metadata. Use responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 cols tablet, 3 cols desktop).
Create components/features/feature-card.tsx - 'use client' interactive card component with framer-motion hover animations, proper focus states, and aria attributes

Create app/how-it-works/page.tsx - Visual workflow page showing the pipeline: Idea -> Specification -> Task Planning -> Implementation -> Testing -> Review -> Pull Request -> Deployment. Use a step-by-step visual layout with connecting lines/arrows. Each step should have an icon, title, and description. Consider a vertical timeline on mobile and horizontal on desktop. Use framer-motion for scroll-triggered animations (stagger children). Export metadata.
Create components/how-it-works/workflow-step.tsx - 'use client' component for individual workflow steps with animation
Run bun run build to verify all three new routes compile
acceptance_criteria: bun run build succeeds with /about, /features, and /how-it-works routes listed
About page has introduction, philosophy, spec-driven development, and AI agent sections
Features page shows 10 feature cards in responsive grid with hover interactions
How It Works page shows 8-step workflow with visual connections
All pages have proper metadata exports
All interactive elements use framer-motion with reduced-motion support
Semantic HTML used throughout (section, article, h1-h3, etc.)
verification: Run 'bun run build' and confirm /about, /features, /how-it-works routes appear
findings: All three pages build successfully. Used client content components (about-content, features-content, how-it-works-content) to keep page.tsx files as server components for metadata exports. worker_threads warnings are non-fatal as expected. Routes /about, /features, /how-it-works all appear in build output.
